channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Kris Jenkins, the Villanova hero in 2016 national title game, sues NCAA for money he could have made
item.description: Kris Jenkins, who made the winning shot for Villanova in the 2016 college basketball championship game, is suing the NCAA and six conferences to recoup income he contends he would have earned if athletes at the time were not barred from making money from their name, image and likeness.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Astros' Spencer Arrighetti breaks thumb in freak batting practice accident
item.description: Houston Astros starting pitcher Spencer Arrighetti suffered an unfortunate injury in a freak batting practice accident on Monday prior to the team's game against the Mariners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arsenal hosts Real Madrid in 'biggest night' of Arteta's career
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It's been 19 years since Arsenal played Real Madrid in the Champions League. That was also the last time Arsenal came close to winning the competition, losing the 2006 final to Barcelona after beating Madrid in the round of 16.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Two courts: NCAA's present (Gators!) and future play out 1700 miles apart on the same day
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The final buzzer in San Antonio closed a drama that ended with confetti and Gator chomps — a thrill-a-minute NCAA title for the Florida Gators that reminded us all of what's so good about the games these college athletes play.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: High school softball rankings, April 8, 2025: Bay Area News Group top 20
item.description: Bay Area high school softball upsets shake up local rankings as Mitty, Notre Dame-Belmont, Mills, Acalanes, Live Oak, Valley Christian, California move up in latest edition
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Retzlaff poised for another big year at BYU
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Last season, BYU quarterback Jake Retzlaff threw for almost 3,000 yards, 20 touchdowns, while rushing for over 400 yards and six TD's as the Cougars shocked everyone with an 11-2 season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Houston's Kelvin Sampson was close to his 1st national title. It slipped away painfully vs. Florida
item.description: Kelvin Sampson watched his Houston team come within seconds of the first NCAA title for both himself and the program.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Beard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump threatens more tariffs on China as global markets shudder over how much pain economy can take
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Undeterred by a panicked stock market, President Donald Trump threatened additional tariffs on China on Monday, raising fresh concerns that his drive to rebalance the global economy could intensify a financially destructive trade war.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Sox's Jarren Duran opens up about mental health, attempted suicide in Netflix documentary
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — EDITOR’S NOTE — This story includes discussion of suicide. If you or someone you know needs help, the national suicide and crisis lifeline in the U.S. is available by calling or texting 988. There is also an online chat at 988lifeline.org.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Johnny Gaudreau's wife Meredith announces birth of couple's third child
item.description: Johnny and his brother were killed in New Jersey when they were riding bicycles and struck from behind by a suspected drunk driver.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Xander Schauffele is on the mend and motivated as ever at the Masters
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Two majors made Xander Schauffele believe he was capable of winning them all. Two months out of golf with a rib injury is putting that belief to the test. Schauffele is more than capable of winning the Masters.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Practice is suspended at the Masters because of rain and a threat of severe storms
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Practice at the Masters was suspended late Monday morning and spectators were pulled off the golf course because of worsening rain and the threat of thunderstorms.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump gushes over Shohei Ohtani as president welcomes Dodgers to White House
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani was the talk of the East Room of the White House as President Donald Trump touted the star player's accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McLaren can be beaten, but Verstappen must be perfect: 5 takeaways from F1’s Japanese Grand Prix
item.description: McLaren remains the team to beat in Formula 1, but Max Verstappen’s victorious drive for Red Bull at the Japanese Grand Prix showed only excellence is good enough. Ahead of this week’s Bahrain Grand Prix, here are five takeaways from Suzuka: So, McLaren can be beaten.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ELLINGWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL personality Annie Agar suffers black eye, concussion after scary fall
item.description: NFL personality Annie Agar revealed she suffered a black eye and a concussion after passing out in her bathroom last week. Agar said it was "not fun.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma softball coach defends players who attended Riley Gaines event
item.description: Oklahoma Sooners softball coach Patty Gasso defended her players attending an event hosted by women's sports activist Riley Gaines last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Keyonte George scores career-high 35 in Jazz loss
item.description: ATLANTA (ABC4 Sports) - Keyonte George set a new career high with 35 points, but it wasn't enough to stop the Utah Jazz' nine-game losing streak in a 147-135 loss to the Atlanta Hawks Sunday. The Jazz have lost 19 of 20 overall. Utah has given up at least 140 points in three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Women's NCAA Final Four tournament a 'slam dunk for Tampa's economy
item.description: Even though the women’s championship is over, Tampa businesses are calling this weekend a "slam dunk." This is the fourth time the city has hosted the NCAA women’s basketball Final Four, and it’s not just basketball fans who are winning.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Geese make nest next to iconic Wrigley Field bleachers during Chicago Cubs games
item.description: The iconic Wrigley Field bleachers welcomed an unusual guest during the Chicago Cubs' series against the San Diego Padres this weekend.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Houston, Florida play for title and put a different spin on the underdog role to wrap March Madness
item.description: Coach Kelvin Sampson’s squad of defenders and deniers face Florida for the national title Monday night. They wrap up a front-runner’s Final Four that featured all No. 1 seeds but ends with the two top ones — Auburn and Duke — sitting at home.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UConn returns to top of women's basketball, dominates South Carolina 82-59 to capture its 12th title
item.description: Dawn Staley and South Carolina hope to finish off a second straight season with an NCAA championship, which would give the Gamecocks a third title in four years. To do so, they'll need to beat Paige Bueckers and UConn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Alex Ovechkin breaks Wayne Gretzky's NHL career goals record by scoring his 895th
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Ovechkin has broken Wayne Gretzky’s record for the most goals in NHL history by scoring the 895th of his career. The Washington Capitals’ captain made history Sunday against the New York Islanders by beating fellow Russian Ilya Sorokin on a power play in the second period.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida makes push all the way to NCAA title game, set to face Houston
item.description: Florida has gone from being picked to finish sixth in the rugged Southeastern Conference to pushing all the way to the final Monday night of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Diego Luna's brace leads RSL past Galaxy, 2-0
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Diego Luna scored two goals as Real Salt Lake cruised past the LA Galaxy Saturday afternoon at America First Field, 2-0. Luna scored the two goals in a five-minute span of the first half and Real Salt Lake (3-4-0, 9 points) controlled the rest of the contest.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Carson Kelly homers as the Cubs beat the Padres 7-1 for their 5th straight win
item.description: Carson Kelly homered and drove in four runs, and the Chicago Cubs beat the San Diego Padres 7-1 on Saturday for their fifth consecutive win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carmelo Anthony and Sue Bird highlight Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Fox 5

item.title: The Final Four again has multiple 5th-year starters. This time, it's a farewell to an uncommon era
item.description: “Credit to the NCAA for giving us a year back because that COVID year, that wasn’t real, honestly. Nah, it wasn’t fun at all.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Strong, Fudd help UConn blow out UCLA 85-51 in Final Four as Bueckers moves 1 win from elusive title
item.description: Sarah Strong and Azzi Fudd didn’t even need much help from superstar Paige Bueckers to propel UConn into the national title game, leading the Huskies to a remarkably easy 85-51 victory over UCLA on Friday night in the Final Four of the women’s NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Doug Feinberg, Fox 5
